title: Parkio Daily Briefing for April 27, 2026
teaser: Toy Story 5 merchandise launches today, plus construction updates at Hollywood Studios and Disneyland. Parks operating normally.

bignews.title: Toy Story 5 merchandise collection launches ahead of June film release
bignews.body: Disney has launched a full collection of Toy Story 5 products today, including interactive talking figures from Mattel, LEGO playsets, Crocs boots inspired by Jessie, and collectibles from Funko and POP MART. The merchandise features classic characters Woody, Buzz, and Jessie, plus new characters Lilypad and Smarty Pants ahead of the film's June 19 theatrical release.

topstories.title: Hollywood Studios construction continues despite rainy weather
topstories.body: Construction work on the Muppets coaster and other updates at Disney's Hollywood Studios is progressing today, with crews working through intermittent rain. The Muppets-themed attraction is part of ongoing expansion in the park.

topstories.title: Disneyland testing new entrance technology
topstories.body: Disneyland has begun testing new technology at park entrances, marking the latest update to guest entry systems. Specific details about the new tech have not yet been disclosed by Disney.

topstories.title: Yak & Yeti introduces new menu items at Animal Kingdom
topstories.body: The popular Asian-fusion restaurant Yak & Yeti at Disney's Animal Kingdom has rolled out new menu items for guests to try. The updates provide fresh dining options at the Anandapur-themed location.

icymi.title: Away travel brand launches Toy Story 5 luggage collection
icymi.body: Away will release a Toy Story 5 travel collection on June 9, featuring rolling luggage, backpacks, and bag charms inspired by Woody, Buzz, Jessie, Aliens, and Lilypad. The collection offers travel essentials for both kids and adults.

icymi.title: Disney Springs restaurant showdown highlights waterfront dining
icymi.body: A new comparison review puts The Boathouse and Paddlefish head-to-head at Disney Springs, helping guests choose between the two popular waterfront dining destinations.

spotlight.title: Universal Orlando updates and new dining options roll out
spotlight.body: Universal Orlando Resort continues to add new food offerings and construction updates across its parks. Recent videos show progress on multiple projects and expanded dining choices for guests visiting the resort this spring.

videos.title: Universal Studios 2026 | New Food, Park Updates & Ride Construction | Universal Orlando Resort Guide
videos.summary: Paging Mr. Morrow provides a comprehensive guide to Universal Orlando's latest food additions and ongoing ride construction updates across the resort.

videos.title: Disney Springs Restaurant Challenge: The Boathouse vs. Paddlefish- Which is Better?
videos.summary: WrightDownMainStreet compares two popular Disney Springs waterfront restaurants to help guests decide which dining experience is best.

videos.summary: ResortTV1 brings viewers a live stream from Magic Kingdom for a Monday tour of the park.

videos.summary: Super Enthused covers Hollywood Studios updates including Muppets coaster construction and park conditions during rainy weather.
